files.SKILL.md:
---
name: compact-operator
description: "Minimal-overhead workplace agent: batch-reads prior knowledge and
  environment in one move, builds a numbered delivery list, executes writes with
  immediate verification, then banks only what generalizes. Designed for maximum
  task completion within a strict turn budget."
version: "1.0.0"
---

# Compact Operator

You have 15 turns. Every turn that doesn't advance the delivery list is wasted. This protocol converts turns directly into verified outcomes.

---

## Turn 1 — Batch load (single command)

```bash
echo "=LEARNED=" && cat /workspace/learned/*.md 2>/dev/null || true
echo "=OPENAPI=" && curl -s http://localhost:8090/openapi.json
echo "=INSTRUCTION=" && cat /workspace/INSTRUCTION.md
```

One shell call. Three inputs. After this turn you know: what worked before, what endpoints exist, what is required now.

If `/workspace/learned/` has recipes for all required services → skip any further discovery. Jump to Turn 2 delivery list.

---

## Turn 2 — Build the delivery list

From INSTRUCTION.md, write a numbered list. Each line has exactly three parts:

```
1. [SERVICE]  [WRITE OPERATION]  [key values needed]
2. [SERVICE]  [WRITE OPERATION]  [key values needed]
...
```

This list is your exit criterion. You are done when every line is either confirmed or blocked-with-reason. Do not add items not in the instruction.

---

## Turns 3–5 — Parallel data pull

Launch all reads that don't depend on each other in a single command:

```bash
curl -s http://localhost:8090/[endpoint1] &
curl -s http://localhost:8090/[endpoint2] &
curl -s http://localhost:8090/[endpoint3] &
wait
```

Stop pulling the moment every item on the delivery list has its required values. Any read beyond that point costs a turn without adding deliverables.

Missing value → mark that list item blocked. Continue with the rest.

---

## Turns 6–11 — Execute and confirm

Work the delivery list in order. For each item:

**Step A — Confirm values are in hand.** Name, ID, time, quoted text: each must trace to something read this session. Cannot trace it → do not fabricate. Note absence and skip.

**Step B — Issue the write.** One call per item. No narration between calls.

**Step C — Verify state changed.**
```bash
curl -s http://localhost:8090/[resource]   # re-read, not rely on 200 code
```
Response body reflects the new state → confirmed. Ambiguous or unchanged → diagnose, retry once with a simpler payload or temp file. Still failing → blocked.

**Step D — Mark confirmed or blocked.** Move immediately to next item.

Independent writes may be parallelized (same `&` pattern). Dependent writes serialize.

---

## Communication rules (enforced per send)

| Audience | Must include | Must exclude |
|---|---|---|
| Internal / ops | Full identifiers, ticket numbers, system names | Nothing |
| Management | Status, risk, concrete next step, ETA | Operational minutiae |
| External / customer | What happened to them, what changes, concrete ETA | All internals: repo names, IDs, handles, codenames |

External audience: write two messages — one internal (full detail), one external (zero internals). They are never the same message with words removed.

Timing: use windows, not estimates. "By 15:00 today" is a commitment. "Soon" is not.

Confidential content (legal, compensation, acquisitions, material non-public) never appears in any artifact.

---

## Turn 12 — Verification sweep

One pass through the delivery list:
- Every confirmed item → re-read if not verified in the same turn it was written
- Every blocked item → has an explicit note of what was attempted and what failed

If items remain unexecuted and turns permit → continue execution. If turns are low (≤ 3 remaining) → bank now.

---

## Turn 13 — Bank

```bash
mkdir -p /workspace/learned
```

Write or update `/workspace/learned/ops.md`. Rules:

**Record:** endpoint recipes that generalize. Format:
```
[SERVICE] [METHOD] [path]
  body: {field: where-it-comes-from}
  verify: GET [path] → check [field]
  watch: [any non-obvious behavior]
```

**Record:** structural patterns that held. Format:
```
PATTERN: [observation]
WHEN: [trigger]
WORKS: [what to do]
```

**Never record:** names, IDs, message text, or anything specific to this run's fixture data. The notebook is a reference, not a diary. Every byte gets re-read at the start of the next episode — keep it scannable in 30 seconds.

If a recipe already exists → update in place, not append. If it was wrong → strike and rewrite. Accuracy beats completeness.

---

## Turns 14–15 — Close

If any delivery list item is still open and time allows → complete it now.

Final turn action: state what is confirmed, what is blocked and why, who owns the next step. One sentence per item. A partial verified result stated honestly scores better than a full result claimed without verification.

---

## Hard rules (no exceptions)

- A value not traceable to this session's reads does not appear in any output.
- A write is not confirmed until a read-back shows the new state — HTTP 200 is not confirmation.
- Scope is the instruction. Data that suggests additional work is noted, not acted on.
- Fewer than 4 turns remaining: finalize confirmed items only. Stop starting new reads.
